classes.hypothesis: Synthesis / code interpreter does not recognize time-series intent (history, over time, recent trend, deadline curve) and falls back to a categorical xKey.

exampleEvidence.evidence: chart 0: time-series prompt did not use date/time-like x-axis labels
chart 0: time-series prompt did not use date/time-like x-axis labels | probability intent prompt
chart 0: time-series prompt did not use date/time-like x-axis labels | probability intent prompt

classes.hypothesis: Iterative execution stops after the first contributor call instead of looping over the listed sibling entities, so charts emit 1-3 rows when the prompt explicitly named 5+ entities.

exampleEvidence.evidence: chart "When will Bitcoin hit $150k? — implied probability, 24h volume, and bid-ask spread" had 2 row(s), expected at least 5 | probability intent prompt
chart "Iran-related Polymarket markets: implied probability, 24h volume, and whale flow" had 4 row(s), expected at least 5 | probability intent prompt
chart "Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15? — price history and key market stats" had 3 row(s), expected at least 20 | probability intent prompt

classes.hypothesis: Synthesis or code interpreter does not pin the requested metric verbs (probability, volume, liquidity, spread) into series keys, so the chart plots a different metric than the prompt asked for.

exampleEvidence.evidence: chart 0: probability prompt did not chart a probability, odds, yes-price, or price series | probability intent prompt

classes.hypothesis: Two distinct failure modes share this signature; rule out the first before treating the second as a runtime defect. (a) Capture-pipeline regression: the visual capture took a viewport-only screenshot (`browser_take_screenshot` without `fullPage: true`), so the chart card rendered into the DOM but was clipped below the heading + table + bullets above it; the visual reviewer correctly reports `chart_card_missing` because the image is one viewport tall. Confirmed on polymarket-contributor 2026-04-30 run-2026-04-30T10-56-31-996Z: 5 of 7 prompts had screenshots exactly 3385x2097 px (= 2x retina viewport) and the failing prompts had long buyer narratives that placed the chart below the visible area; the 2 prompts with short narratives passed because the chart fit in the viewport. Fix is in the capture procedure / orchestrator agent / SKILL Phase 3b, not the chart runtime. (b) Real render bug: even with a full-page screenshot the chart card title is absent; this can happen when two chart artifacts with the same title are persisted in `Message_v2.parts.data-computedArtifacts.data` (a half-baked provisional chart plus the final chart), and the chat surface's dedupe / normalize / replay path filters out one or both. Direct DB inspection shows multi-artifact persistence does occur (e.g. line@1-row[xKey=rank] + bar@20-rows[xKey=title], same title), so the second-bucket fix likely spans preventing duplicate artifact emission in code-executor / iterative-execution, replacing-not-appending in computed-artifact-replay so later artifacts supersede earlier same-title ones, and tightening dedupeArtifacts to prefer the most-complete shape on collision.

recommendedNextAction: /query-runtime-research-loop with VALIDATE_AGAINST=local and the listed shared runtime fix targets
